name: Green bell pepper (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight (concentration_mM computed assuming ~0.1 L per 100 g, density ~1)

components.name: water
components.evidence: Matrix is ~94% water; dilute aqueous medium.

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Co-dominant free sugar; primary fermentable carbon source alongside fructose.

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Co-dominant free sugar (glucose~=fructose in green/unripe pepper).

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Minor in green (unripe) pepper; rises on ripening to red.

components.name: maltose
components.evidence: Not detected.

components.name: galactose
components.evidence: Not detected.

components.name: starch
components.evidence: Green bell pepper is essentially starch-free (0 g).

components.name: dietary fiber
components.evidence: Cell-wall polysaccharide; largely non-fermentable by most GEMs.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Zhang et al. 2022, Front. Nutr. PMC9871545 (green pepper C. annuum)
components.evidence: Dominant organic acid, 39-64% of total organic acids; range 1.82-5.60 mg/g FW, midpoint used.

components.name: malic acid
components.evidence: Second/third organic acid, 12.7-21.6% of total; range 0.99-1.88 mg/g FW, midpoint used.

components.name: fumaric acid
components.evidence: Reported 1.48-3.71 mg/g FW (18.8-33.8% of total organic acids) in green pepper; midpoint used. Unusually high vs other databases - flag as cultivar/method dependent.

components.name: alpha-ketoglutarate
components.evidence: 0.70-1.43 mg/g FW (10.4-16.8% of total); TCA-cycle intermediate, midpoint used.

components.name: oxalic acid
components.evidence: Minor, <0.7 mg/g FW (<8% of total); approximate.

components.name: L-ascorbate
components.evidence: Vitamin C; green bell pepper is a rich source (~80 mg/100g).

components.name: dehydroascorbate
components.evidence: Oxidized form of ascorbate; present, unquantified.

components.name: protein (total)
components.evidence: Low total protein; amino-acid profile below is the hydrolysate/free pool.

components.name: L-aspartate
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid in pepper.

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Second most abundant; umami/N source.

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: Dominant cation.

components.name: phosphate (as P)
components.evidence: Phosphorus content, mM as elemental P.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Consolidates the FooDB Fe2+/Fe3+/'Iron (Fe3+)'/'Fe2+ mitochondria' duplicates into one measured Fe.

components.name: selenium
components.evidence: Reported 0 in USDA; keep as trace/null.

components.name: fluoride
components.evidence: Measured trace; retained.

components.name: chloride
components.evidence: Counter-ion, present, unquantified in USDA panel.

components.name: sulfate
components.evidence: S source, unquantified.

components.name: vitamin C
components.evidence: Same molecule as L-ascorbate above; listed for the vitamin panel.

components.name: nicotinate (B3)
components.evidence: Niacin.

components.name: folate (B9)
components.evidence: DFE.

components.name: glycine betaine
components.evidence: Betaine, trace.

components.name: gamma-tocopherol
components.evidence: Present, unquantified in USDA.

components.name: vitamin A (RAE)
components.evidence: From provitamin-A carotenoids; retinol itself = 0.

components.name: retinol
components.evidence: 0 preformed retinol (plant food).

components.name: vitamin D
components.evidence: Absent in plant food.

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: Provitamin-A carotenoid.

components.name: lutein+zeaxanthin
components.evidence: USDA reports lutein+zeaxanthin combined.

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Dominant fatty acid; total fat only 0.17 g/100g.

remove.name: Silver
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant metal, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Arsenate
remove.reason: toxic contaminant; not a nutrient of pepper

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: trace element, not a defined growth nutrient of this food matrix

remove.name: Creatine cytosol
remove.reason: animal muscle metabolite; biologically implausible in a plant food; FooDB predicted

remove.name: Taurine C2H7NO3S
remove.reason: animal metabolite; implausible in a plant food; FooDB predicted

remove.name: 17alpha-ethynylestradiol
remove.reason: synthetic pharmaceutical estrogen; contaminant/predicted artifact, not a pepper constituent

remove.name: N-Methylaniline
remove.reason: industrial aromatic amine; implausible food constituent / contaminant

remove.name: Bromine
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace element, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Solanidine
remove.reason: potato/nightshade glycoalkaloid aglycone; implausible/negligible in sweet bell pepper, likely FooDB mis-assignment

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: duplicate of measured iron (consolidated into single 'iron' entry)

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: compartment-tagged duplicate of iron; artifact of FooDB/BiGG tagging

missing_added: citric acid (dominant organic acid, was absent)
keep_uncovered: Capsianoside I-IV / II / III / C / D / E
genuine Capsicum phytochemicals with no BiGG metabolite
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 170427 (Peppers, sweet, green, raw)
Zhang et al. 2022, Frontiers in Nutrition 9:1027605, PMC9871545 (organic acids & sugars of green pepper, Capsicum annuum)
notes: Green (unripe) sweet bell pepper is a ~94% water, low-sugar matrix. Dominant fermentable carbon = free glucose ~= fructose (~1.1-1.2 g/100g each; sucrose minor), plus a substantial organic-acid pool led by citric acid (~0.37 g/100g) with malic, fumaric and alpha-ketoglutarate; these organic acids were entirely missing from the FooDB profile and are the key correction. Very rich in vitamin C (~80 mg/100g). Capsaicinoids are near-zero in the non-pungent sweet bell pepper but retained as uncovered phytochemicals. Fumaric-acid value flagged as high/cultivar-dependent.
